ontology.foodon_gap_note: FoodOn has a 'chicken nugget' class (FOODON_03315091) but does not model the full meal combo (nuggets + fries + drink). Matched to the primary protein component.
ontology.foodex2_gap_note: FoodEx2 code A0CKP covers breaded fried chicken nuggets as the dominant component. The full meal combo (with fries and soft drink) has no single FoodEx2 code; the nugget component is used as the primary anchor.
ontology.snomed_ct_gap_note: No SNOMED CT concept maps precisely to a fast-food chicken nuggets meal combo. SNOMED CT is not used here to avoid fabrication.

serving.description: 1 meal set (6 nuggets + medium fries + medium soft drink)
serving.natural_language_portion: One standard meal set comprising 6 chicken nuggets (approximately 105 g), a medium portion of French fries (approximately 115 g), and a medium soft drink (approximately 270 g), totalling approximately 490 g.

nutrient_source.title: 6Pc Chicken Nuggets Meal (D) Original — Singapore HPB Foodo Nutrient Database
nutrient_source.source_match_notes: Nutrient values sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database entry for '6Pc Chicken Nuggets Meal (D) Original' (cr_id G00010013). All per-100g macros, sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol, and sugar values are taken verbatim from the HPB record. Saturated fat and dietary fibre are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for breaded fried chicken nuggets and fast-food French fries (USDA SR Legacy #21066 and #21137) where HPB does not report these fields, and this is documented in notes.

gi_source.title: International tables of glycemic index and glycemic load values: 2021 — Atkinson et al.; and University of Sydney GI Database analogous entries for breaded fried chicken nuggets and French fries
gi_source.organization: University of Sydney GI Database; American Journal of Clinical Nutrition
gi_source.source_match_notes: No direct GI measurement exists for this specific HPB meal combo. GI estimated as 72 (high) based on the dominant high-GI components: French fries (GI ~75, University of Sydney GI Database) and wheat-breaded fried chicken nuggets (GI ~66, analogous breaded fried chicken, University of Sydney GI Database). The soft drink component (cola, GI ~63) contributes additional glycaemic load. A weighted composite GI of approximately 72 is used. Glycaemic load per serving calculated as: GI 72 × available carbohydrate per serving (14.3 g/100g × 490 g serving / 100) = 72 × 14.3 × 4.9 / 100 ≈ 10.1 g GL per serving (nuggets component only; full meal GL is substantially higher when fries and drink carbohydrates are included at full serving weight).

hypertension.key_factor: Sodium 708 mg/100 g — exceeds the 600 mg/100 g hard gate; deep-fried preparation and sugar-sweetened beverage compound cardiovascular sodium burden
hypertension.guidance: Patients with hypertension should avoid this meal; if consumed, replace the soft drink with water, request no added salt on fries, and limit or omit dipping sauces, as the combined sodium load from nuggets, fries, and sauces substantially exceeds the 2,000 mg/day DASH target in a single sitting.

type2_diabetes.key_factor: High glycaemic index (~72), refined-carbohydrate French fries, sugar-sweetened beverage, and low dietary fibre (1.0 g/100 g) create a high glycaemic load incompatible with postprandial glucose control per ADA guidelines
type2_diabetes.guidance: Patients with Type 2 Diabetes should avoid this meal combination; if chicken nuggets are consumed in isolation, portion size should be limited to 3 pieces, the soft drink must be replaced with water or a non-caloric beverage, and fries should be substituted with a side salad to reduce the overall glycaemic load.

cardiovascular_disease.key_factor: Estimated saturated fat 5.2 g/100 g, trans fat 0.2 g/100 g, cholesterol 50 mg/100 g, and sodium 708 mg/100 g; deep-frying generates advanced glycation end-products (AGEs) that promote endothelial inflammation
cardiovascular_disease.guidance: Patients with cardiovascular disease should avoid this meal; the combination of saturated fat, trans fat, high sodium, and deep-fried AGEs is strongly atherogenic, and regular consumption is inconsistent with AHA dietary guidelines for cardiovascular risk reduction.

rheumatoid_arthritis.key_factor: Deep-frying generates pro-inflammatory AGEs; trans fat (~0.2 g/100 g) is strongly pro-inflammatory; sodium 708 mg/100 g worsens RA-related tissue oedema
rheumatoid_arthritis.guidance: Patients with rheumatoid arthritis should avoid deep-fried foods such as this meal; the AGE content, trans fat, and high sodium collectively worsen joint inflammation and tissue swelling, and the soft drink adds further refined sugar that promotes systemic inflammation.

llm_training.natural_language_description: The 6-Piece Chicken Nuggets Meal (Original) is a fast-food combo meal sold in Singapore, consisting of six breaded and deep-fried chicken nuggets, a medium portion of French fries, and a medium sugar-sweetened carbonated soft drink. The nuggets are made from processed chicken meat coated in a seasoned wheat-flour batter and deep-fried in vegetable oil, yielding a crispy golden exterior with a moist, tender interior. The meal is classified as ultra-processed and is high in total fat (22.6 g/100 g), sodium (708 mg/100 g), and energy (326 kcal/100 g), with moderate protein (16.5 g/100 g) and relatively low carbohydrate from the nugget component alone (14.3 g/100 g). The full meal glycaemic index is estimated at approximately 72 (high) due to the French fries and soft drink components. The meal is halal-certified and widely consumed in Singapore as a quick lunch or dinner option. Clinically, this meal is unsuitable for individuals with hypertension (sodium far exceeds 600 mg/100 g threshold), Type 2 Diabetes (high GI, sugar-sweetened beverage, low fibre), cardiovascular disease (saturated fat, trans fat, AGEs from deep-frying), and rheumatoid arthritis (pro-inflammatory AGEs and trans fat). The HPB Singapore Foodo database is the primary nutrient source for this record.
common_user_queries: How many calories in 6 piece chicken nuggets meal?
Is chicken nuggets meal suitable for diabetics?
Sodium content of chicken nuggets meal Singapore
Chicken nuggets meal nutrition facts Singapore
Can I eat chicken nuggets if I have high blood pressure?

confidence_reason: Core macros, sodium, potassium, calcium, magnesium, iron, cholesterol, and sugar are sourced directly from the HPB Singapore Foodo database (cr_id G00010013, confidence 0.95). Saturated fat (5.2 g/100 g), dietary fibre (1.0 g/100 g), and trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) are estimated from USDA FoodData Central analogous entries for breaded fried chicken nuggets and fast-food French fries and are documented in notes. GI is an analogous estimate from the University of Sydney GI Database. Overall confidence is high for the HPB-sourced fields.
notes: 1. Saturated fat (5.2 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central SR Legacy entry #21066 (Chicken nuggets, breaded, frozen, prepared) and #21137 (Fast foods, potato, french fried) as HPB does not report saturated fat for this record. 2. Dietary fibre (1.0 g/100 g) is estimated from the same USDA analogous entries; HPB does not report fibre for this record. 3. Trans fat (0.2 g/100 g) is estimated from USDA FoodData Central data for commercially deep-fried breaded chicken products; HPB does not report trans fat. 4. monounsaturated_fat_g, polyunsaturated_fat_g, water_g, vitamin_c_mg, vitamin_a_mcg_rae, vitamin_b1_thiamine_mg, vitamin_b2_riboflavin_mg, vitamin_b3_niacin_mg, vitamin_b6_mg, vitamin_b12_mcg, folate_mcg_dfe, vitamin_d_mcg, vitamin_e_mg, phosphorus_mg, zinc_mg, selenium_mcg are set to -1: detailed fatty acid fractions, vitamin panel, phosphorus, zinc, and selenium data for this specific HPB meal combo are not available in the HPB Foodo database or USDA FoodData Central as of 2026-04-16. 5. GI of 72 is an analogous composite estimate based on University of Sydney GI Database values for French fries (~75) and breaded fried chicken (~66); no direct GI measurement exists for this specific meal combo. 6. The meal combo includes a sugar-sweetened soft drink which triggers the SSB hard gate for diabetes suitability assessment. 7. Serving size of 490 g is an estimate based on typical fast-food meal combo weights (6 nuggets ~105 g + medium fries ~115 g + medium drink ~270 g); actual weights may vary by outlet.
